config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama agar kepentingan masyarakat tidak berubah menjadi alasan untuk menindas orang yang lemah atau berbeda pendapat. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai dengan menghormati martabat, kebebasan, dan perlindungan hukum setiap orang. Kalau hak individu mudah dikalahkan, yang sering terjadi justru penyalahgunaan kuasa atas nama “demi masyarakat”.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over the individual in any blanket way. From an American perspective, that kind of statement makes me wary because history shows how easily “the public good” can be used to trample free speech, due process, minority rights, and personal liberty. Society does matter, but its legitimate goals have to be limited by individual rights, otherwise you get a system where people can be sacrificed whenever the majority or the state says it is convenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu secara umum. Dari pengalaman di Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, bahkan kadang menekan kelompok kecil atau orang yang tidak punya kuasa. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi justru akan lebih sehat kalau dibangun di atas perlindungan hak dasar, bukan dengan mengorbankannya. Menurut saya, masyarakat kuat itu bukan yang paling mudah mengalahkan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi individu tanpa kehilangan rasa tanggung jawab sosial.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not accept the idea that society should generally outrank the individual, because in the United States that usually ends up meaning the powerful get to define what “society” wants. Public safety and shared rules matter, but they are only legitimate when they are constrained by rights like free speech, due process, and equal protection. I also think the argument that the individual must yield for the common good is often used too loosely, and once you normalize that, it becomes easy to excuse abuse against unpopular people. A healthy society protects people first and then asks them to contribute, not the other way around.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus mengalahkan hak individu secara umum. Dari pengalaman di Indonesia, label “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang tidak proporsional, dan ujungnya justru merugikan warga yang paling lemah atau tidak punya akses kuasa. Kepentingan bersama itu penting, tetapi legitim kalau dibangun di atas perlindungan hak dasar, bukan dengan menjadikan individu sebagai korban yang bisa dikorbankan seenaknya. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat adalah masyarakat yang bisa menertibkan kebutuhan bersama tanpa menghapus martabat dan kebebasan orang per orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think the interests of society should take priority over individual rights as a general rule. From a United States perspective, I am very skeptical of any broad claim that “the common good” should come first, because that language is often used to justify overreach by the government or the majority against unpopular people. Public safety and social responsibility matter, but they only stay legitimate when they are limited by rights like free speech, due process, and equal protection. A strong society is not one that can sacrifice individuals easily; it is one that protects individual dignity while still asking people to contribute to the common good.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
